actionUnits.condition: GvrTooltip (script fileID 950, GUID 7c52851ea0de74a228fa29a84de008ba) is configured with location=3 (AppButtonInside), text=fileID 1015, alwaysVisible=false. We want to validate runtime behavior when the controller handedness and arm model update occur (GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate) so the tooltip flips sides, updates rotation/alignment, and alpha follows tooltipAlphaValue. Since there is no Collider/Rigidbody/EventTrigger on this GameObject and no UnityEvents (m_Calls) are defined, we directly invoke available public no-parameter methods to exercise logic and retrieve state needed for assertions. Expected: IsTooltipInside() returns true for location=AppButtonInside; IsTooltipOnLeft() returns true when handedness is Right (inside) and false when handedness is Left (outside), and UI elements have rotations/alignment adjusted accordingly after OnArmModelUpdate.

Manually invoke public, parameterless methods on GvrTooltip (MonoBehaviour component id 929, script guid 7c52851ea0de74a228fa29a84de008ba) to validate logic for location=2 (AppButtonOutside) and alwaysVisible=false. No m_Calls found; object lacks Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger, so direct method invocation is used to achieve coverage.

Trigger GvrAudioSource playback manually since playOnAwake=0. MonoBehaviour id=973 uses script guid=6dbca64e524ea44f9b26142ec59aca8a (GvrAudioSource). Serialized clip guid=ec4642a7ec8557245a62d08bb0076fd9; loop=0; volume=1. Expected: Play initializes source and starts audio; then Pause pauses playback.
Resume the paused GvrAudioSource and then stop to cover UnPause() and Stop() paths, including ShutdownSource().

Call GvrAudioSource.Play() on MonoBehaviour component id 944 (script GUID 6dbca64e524ea44f9b26142ec59aca8a). Scene config: playOnAwake=false, clip assigned (guid 3b1bac9d0cdfad24e9ffb75aa75e4f96), loop=false, volume=1.0. Note: GameObject has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger; framework may need explicit permission to invoke methods.
After playback has started, pause the GvrAudioSource to cover Pause() branch and isPaused flag.
Resume after pause to exercise UnPause() and related state transitions.
Stop playback to hit Stop() path including ShutdownSource().

Collect attempt on mirror. MonoBehaviour_1 (id: 995) is Collectable (guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e) with public no-arg Collect(). Scene meta shows item: {fileID: 0}, dispatch has no persistent calls. Expect no destruction and no inventory change; call should still execute safely.

Force escape sequence. MonoBehaviour_3 (id: 997) is FindEscape (guid: 039fb8407de0f43eea2f8c130a6df149) with public no-arg Escape(). Usable.dispatch (MonoBehaviour_2 id: 996) has m_PersistentCalls targeting Escape, but Use(inv) requires parameter and is wired via EventTrigger (id: 994). To cover the configured m_Calls, invoke Escape() directly and verify: mirror-tagged object audio plays, reflectionProbe is destroyed, Usable/Collectable/Collider on mirror are disabled, background music stops, related coroutines stop.

Force flower placement sequence. MonoBehaviour_2 (id: 1011) maps to ReplaceFlower (guid: b9a7c98e07a2a4348a8fa4e52d5fd6af). PlaceFlower() activates tagged 'flower' (id: 82), updates mirror's Usable.inspectMessage (mirror id: 264, Usable id: 996), and disables vase's Collectable/Usable/Collider. EventTrigger (id: 1010) normally drives Use(inv), but PlaceFlower is a public no-arg method suitable for direct triggering.

Validate mirror message updated by PlaceFlower(). MonoBehaviour_2 (id: 996) on mirror is Usable (guid: 164fdbff0d3254b5691f0f3dddc50068) inheriting from Inspectable with public no-arg Inspect(). Expect to show updated text: "The flower isn't in the reflection..."

Call Inspectable.Inspect() on MonoBehaviour_1 (id: 899, guid: 20f309bd30f854a1cb08cabddfbc74e5) attached to GameObject 'default' (fileID: 27). BoxCollider (id: 776) enabled; no Rigidbody present. This validates default Inspect flow before any Lightable state changes.

Toggle light ON via Lightable.ToggleLight() on MonoBehaviour_2 (id: 900, guid: 88bc783c768ce4eb29e7f2d95dc4f793). Start() is auto-invoked by Unity; manual Start is not triggered. This should enable the child Light and set Inspectable.inspectMessage to "That's much better...".

Toggle light OFF via Lightable.ToggleLight() on MonoBehaviour_2 (id: 900) to cover both branches. This should disable the Light and set Inspectable.inspectMessage to "Hello darkness, my old friend...".

Pick up object via Collectable.Collect() on MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 898, guid: fd9b592feb7384d0a9c94749396c5a5e). Scene shows item: null; inventory.AddItem(item) will be skipped. Invoke DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() to drive hour increments and hit wrap-around. MonoBehaviour id=925 on GameObject 'Hours' (fileID=92) maps to script GUID=99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Serialized config: currentValue=0, maximumValue=24, NextValue=null, tens=fileID 901, units=fileID 1016, tensValue=10, reverse=false. No Rigidbody/Collider present; manual trigger required to exercise logic and downstream DisplaySelectedObject.TurnSelectedWithButton calls.

Manual invocation to exercise hour incrementing and LED display updates. MonoBehaviour id=925 on GameObject 'Hours' (fileID=92) is DigitalClockValue (script GUID=99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec). Serialized: currentValue=0, maximumValue=24, NextValue=null, tens=fileID 901, units=fileID 1016, tensValue=10, reverse=false. MonoBehaviour id=901 on child 'LED Yellow Numbers' (fileID=29) is DisplaySelectedObject (script GUID=1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9) with displayOptions set, currentDisplay=0, ButtonSelectable=false. Calling DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() repeatedly will internally call tens.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue/tensValue) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue%tensValue); this covers DisplaySelectedObject switching across indices (0→1→2) and wrap-around at 24.

Invoke DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() to increment hours and drive LED updates. MonoBehaviour id=925 on GameObject 'Hours' (fileID=92) maps to script GUID=99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Serialized: currentValue=0, maximumValue=24, NextValue=null, tens=fileID 901 (child 'LED Yellow Numbers' fileID=29), units=fileID 1016 (child 'LED Yellow Numbers' fileID=293), tensValue=10, reverse=false. MonoBehaviour id=901 and id=1016 on the two 'LED Yellow Numbers' children both map to script GUID=1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9 (DisplaySelectedObject) with populated displayOptions and currentDisplay=0; OnEnable already ran at scene load. Calling ChangeTimeValue repeatedly exercises: increment path, wrap-around at 24 to 0, and internally calls tens.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue/tensValue) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue%tensValue), covering DisplaySelectedObject switching across indices. No separate manual triggers for DisplaySelectedObject are needed.

Manual invocation to exercise minute incrementing, LED updates, and wrap-around cascading to Hours. MonoBehaviour id=913 on GameObject 'Minutes' (fileID=65) is DigitalClockValue (script GUID=99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec). Serialized config: currentValue=0, maximumValue=60, NextValue=fileID 925 (Hours.DigitalClockValue), tens=fileID 893 (DisplaySelectedObject on Minutes tens LED), units=fileID 895 (DisplaySelectedObject on Minutes units LED), tensValue=10, reverse=false. Repeatedly calling ChangeTimeValue() covers: normal increment path; tens/unity display updates via tens.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue/tensValue) and units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue%tensValue); and overflow at 59→0 which triggers NextValue.ChangeTimeValue() on Hours. DisplaySelectedObject.OnEnable ran at load; no separate manual triggers are needed.

Manual invocation of DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() on GameObject 'LEDPoint' to cover normal increment and wrap-around. MonoBehaviour id=943 uses script GUID=99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec (DigitalClockValue). Serialized config: currentValue=0, maximumValue=2, NextValue=null, tens=null, units=fileID 923 (DisplaySelectedObject), tensValue=2, reverse=false. Expect units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue % tensValue) to be called on each step; wrap occurs when currentValue exceeds maximumValue-1.

Trigger DigitalClockValue.ChangeTimeValue() on GameObject 'LEDPoint' to drive unit display updates on its child 'LED Yellow Point'. MonoBehaviour id=943 is DigitalClockValue (script GUID=99d3f5635e2a2c94bba763a859e816ec) with serialized config: currentValue=0, maximumValue=2, NextValue=null, tens=null, units=fileID 923 (DisplaySelectedObject on 'LED Yellow Point'), tensValue=2, reverse=false. This will call units.TurnSelectedWithButton(currentValue % tensValue) and cover both normal increment and wrap-around branches. MonoBehaviour id=923 is DisplaySelectedObject (script GUID=1bd5b4c54e111e240aa7b6088078e1f9) with displayOptions size=2, currentDisplay=0, temporalGOArrange=0, ButtonSelectable=false; its OnEnable ran at scene load to set oldDisplay=currentDisplay; subsequent calls exercise TurnSelectedWithButton path.

Scene MonoBehaviours match scripts: GvrArmModel (MonoBehaviour id=948, guid=7dda4bb2d5509e44e978d2bf56e25d7c) with settings addedElbowHeight=0, addedElbowDepth=0, pointerTiltAngle=15, fadeDistanceFromFace=0.32, tooltipMinDistanceFromFace=0.45, followGaze=DuringMotion(1), useAccelerometer=false(0); and GvrController (MonoBehaviour id=949, guid=adde9bdea083943199c7959004756e60) with emulatorConnectionMode=USB(1). No Collider/EventTrigger/Rigidbody present. The GvrController EndOfFrame coroutine auto-invokes OnControllerUpdate each frame, which in turn triggers GvrArmModel.OnControllerUpdate via subscription in GvrArmModel.Start. This step waits briefly to allow automatic events to run; no public no-arg methods are available to call manually.

Manually invoke Inventory.ToggleVisibility() on MonoBehaviour fileID 985 (guid 3652d147b24254c618ce4989e3b9003c) to simulate GvrController.AppButtonUp. Expects CanvasGroup alpha and blocksRaycasts to toggle between 0/false and 1/true. No m_Calls defined in scene for this object.

Target MonoBehaviour ShowMessage (script guid 6e888d82ad62a4fd99699eeee0553755, MonoBehaviour id 939) has only one public method RunMessage(string), which requires a parameter. MessageText has no Collider/Rigidbody/EventTrigger and no UnityEvent m_Calls configured in scene, so it cannot be triggered automatically via scene events or by a parameterless public method. To cover the coroutine ShowText, we need a UI event (e.g., Button.onClick) or another script to invoke RunMessage at runtime with a test string (and ensure a CanvasGroup exists on the parent to observe fade in/out: alpha -> 1, wait 5s, alpha -> 0). Please provide the invoker object (fileID) or an exposed parameterless wrapper method.

Player (fileID:129) has no Rigidbody and must not be Grabbed/Transformed. MonoBehaviour id 915 on ddcontroller maps to script guid aa952cbcc0eb13d4ca558b6da550ff55 (GvrControllerVisual). Scene settings match script fields: touchPoint(fileID:109), material_idle(3b4f6031d569a624ba87b631c93a788a), material_app(3fe59d3fe0697b84196c28c184197906), material_system(6c9fcd230d4724c40af9e27326dc25f1), material_touchpad(a89bc9f2db5f60743a95275d0336b6c2), touchTransparent(089662c06964d614db131b6b344431e4), touchOpaque(7bc1b31ae424a1448b1be6be3707ad31). GvrControllerVisual exposes no public no-arg methods; its coverage relies on automatic lifecycle (Awake/Start/OnDestroy) and the external event GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate invoking private OnArmModelUpdate(). To achieve full coverage we need a public no-arg method on the parent Controller's GvrArmModel (fileID unknown, parent Transform fileID:527) to force an arm update, plus a way to simulate GvrController states: ClickButton, AppButton, Recentering, IsTouching, TouchPos and adjust GvrControllerVisual.AlphaValue.

MonoBehaviour id 921 on Laser maps to script guid 51b65678ccdd949e9a58874d2880c0ef (GvrLaserPointer). The other attached script MonoBehaviour id 922 maps to guid 4c173f7c6a736fe469d4acc5b108d6d5 (GvrArmModelOffsets) with joint=Pointer(0) and depends on GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate; additional info is needed to force that event.
